sources.notes: Interactive digit-sum query task with k=4 and c=4e18.
Version 2 writeup gives the main residue observation; Version 1 follows the same CRT/interactivity principle with looser query budget.

annotation.summary: Interactively determine a hidden integer by asking digit sums in chosen bases.
annotation.constraints: Only four queries are available and c is 4e18, so the bases must reveal enough modular information at once.
annotation.core_idea: For base b, when x >= b, the digit sum is congruent to x modulo b-1. Choose bases whose b-1 values are coprime and whose product exceeds the search range, while using -1 answers as threshold information for small x. The final answer is reconstructed from residues by CRT and checked through the interactor.
annotation.complexity: O(1) queries and arithmetic per game.

solution_variants.name: crt-bases
solution_variants.summary: Choose bases so digit-sum responses provide residues modulo pairwise coprime b-1 values.

tags.evidence: The solution is an adaptive query strategy against an interactor.
Digit-sum answers give residues modulo b-1, which are combined by CRT.
The main work is constructing a fixed set of useful query bases.
The invariant digit_sum_b(x) == x mod (b-1) drives the strategy.
